summary: Retire the built-in `spec` kind â€” `src/spec.rs`, `extract::spec_features`, and its hardwired import/workspace/drift plumbing â€” now the data-declared `spec` kind covers it.

per.section: Decision: a custom kind is declared data, never engine code

edit.description: Drop `spec_features` and its unit tests â€” a custom kind extracts via the data algebra, not a bespoke projector.
Drop the residual built-in spec plumbing (`import_spec`, any remaining `discover_spec_files` surface) not already removed by KIND-IMPORT-DISCOVERY.
Remove the `Workspace.specs` field and the built-in `Spec::from_surface_dir` load path (and its tests); custom-kind surface loading is generic, not spec-special.
Remove the spec drift axis that reads `workspace.specs` + `discover_spec_files`; reconcile drift to the generic custom-kind path.
Reconcile any end-to-end assertion referencing the built-in spec surface/IR.

tests.asserts: `temper` reads `specs/` solely through the data-declared custom kind â€” the built-in `Spec` IR, `spec_features`, and the spec-special import/workspace/drift plumbing are gone and the suite stays green.

acceptance: The built-in `spec` module and its bespoke plumbing (the `Spec` IR, `spec_features`, `import_spec`, `Workspace.specs`, drift's spec axis) are removed; `temper` reads `specs/` only through the data-declared custom kind; the whole suite is green.
notes: A custom kind shipped as a built-in breaks "temper ships none of them" â€” a stranger installing temper would inherit temper's `spec` kind. Cross-cutting: `Spec` is woven through import, check (`Workspace.specs`), drift (spec axis), extract, lib. Serialized last so specs stay discoverable + checkable via data throughout the chain; confirm no `check`/drift regression over `specs/`.

summary: Reconcile the top-level `[[edge]]` construct into `[kind.<name>.relationships]` so a reference is a kind capability, not a separate construct.

per.section: The harness is a graph too â€” and references are declared edges

edit.description: Move edge parsing from the standalone top-level `[[edge]]` array to `[kind.<name>.relationships]` under the owning kind â€” an edge is the source kind's declared reference field over its declared reference syntax. Preserve the parsed `Edge` shape the graph consumes.
Read the declared relationships off the owning kind rather than a free-standing edge list; route resolution + admissibility behavior unchanged.
Assemble the harness graph from the kinds' relationships instead of `AuthorLayer::edges`.
Update the edge-construction fixtures to the `[kind.<name>.relationships]` shape.

tests.asserts: Edges declared under `[kind.<name>.relationships]` assemble the harness graph and drive route resolution + admissibility exactly as the standalone `[[edge]]` construct did.

acceptance: A reference is declared under its owning kind's `[kind.<name>.relationships]` (over that kind's declared reference syntax) rather than a standalone `[[edge]]` table; the harness graph, route resolution, and edge-admissibility behavior are preserved.
notes: Note (d) of the reconciliation. The graph foundation shipped soundly (`src/graph.rs`, 54eb52f) over `AuthorLayer::edges`; this aligns the edge-declaration *shape* with the specced kind capability. `degree`/`acyclic` (the remaining graph-scope predicates) read the same relationships and follow as later entries; this also unlocks the spec kind's references-resolve clause (backtick-filename syntax).
